findings.evidence: CharFunPosDef.lean:49 `[SeminormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [MeasurableSpace E] [OpensMeasurableSpace E]`; new file line 33 `[NormedAddCommGroup E]`; `IsPositiveDefinite` needs only `[AddMonoid M] [StarAddMonoid M]` (Basic.lean:66,72), all satisfied by a seminormed group.
findings.fix: Split the variable block: state the three positive-definiteness/kernel lemmas under `[SeminormedAddCommGroup E] [InnerProductSpace ℝ E] [MeasurableSpace E] [OpensMeasurableSpace E]` (matching CharFunPosDef.lean:49), and require `NormedAddCommGroup E`/`BorelSpace E`/`SecondCountableTopology E` only for the two `continuous_charFun_and_…` bundles.
findings.issue: The shared variable block forces `NormedAddCommGroup E` on all declarations, but `charFun_isPositiveDefiniteKernel`, `charFun_star_kernel_isPositiveDefiniteKernel_of_star_eq_neg`, and `charFun_isPositiveDefinite_of_star_eq_neg` only use `charFun_posSemidef`/`charFun_fintype_sum_mul_conj_nonneg`, which are stated at `SeminormedAddCommGroup E`. These three are needlessly restricted to the separating-norm case and won't apply where the existing charFun PD API does.

summary: The three non-continuity lemmas are stated at NormedAddCommGroup, stronger than the SeminormedAddCommGroup level of their sole dependencies (charFun_posSemidef / charFun_fintype_sum_mul_conj_nonneg), so they under-generalize relative to the existing charFun API. The star = - hypotheses are genuinely used and at the right level.
